title: Waiting for a train at a small station
summary: Describe a quiet moment of waiting for a train at a small station.
topic: Waiting for a train at a small station
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about waiting for a train at a small station. Say where the station was, what you saw, what you heard, and how you felt. Use past simple.
hintStarters: One winter morning in Sofia, I…
I stood on the platform and I…
sampleText: One winter morning in Sofia, I went to a small train station outside the city. I wanted to visit a friend in a village two hours away. I bought one ticket from a small window. The man behind the glass did not look at me. He pushed the ticket under the window with one hand. The platform was almost empty. Light snow was on the ground. I sat on a cold wooden bench. An old woman sat at the other end with a small dog. She read a newspaper very slowly. A man in a black coat walked up and down. He looked at his watch many times. The train was twenty minutes late. I did not feel angry. I felt strangely calm. I watched the snow on the tracks. I heard a far horn from another train. When my train finally came, the bench was a little warm under me. The old woman folded her newspaper. The man stopped walking. We all stood up together. I will remember that quiet platform for a long time.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Light snow was on the ground
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: có lớp tuyết mỏng trên mặt đất

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a single experience
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể về một lần cụ thể, dùng quá khứ đơn cho mọi hành động: 'went', 'bought', 'sat', 'read', 'walked'. Lưu ý 'read' trong quá khứ phát âm là /red/.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: For a single past experience, use past simple for every action: 'went', 'bought', 'sat', 'read', 'walked'. Note: 'read' in past simple is pronounced /red/.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'almost' as a softening word
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'almost' nghĩa là 'gần như' nhưng chưa hoàn toàn: 'The platform was almost empty' = 'sân ga gần như trống nhưng có vài người'. Đặt trước tính từ, trạng từ, hoặc động từ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'almost' means 'nearly but not quite': 'The platform was almost empty' = the platform was nearly empty but had a few people. Place 'almost' before an adjective, adverb, or verb.

sampleTranslationVi: Một sáng mùa đông ở Sofia, tôi đến một nhà ga nhỏ ngoài thành phố. Tôi muốn thăm một người bạn ở ngôi làng cách hai giờ. Tôi mua một vé tại quầy nhỏ. Người đàn ông phía sau tấm kính không nhìn tôi. Anh ấy đẩy vé qua cửa sổ bằng một tay. Sân ga gần như trống. Có lớp tuyết mỏng trên mặt đất. Tôi ngồi xuống một chiếc ghế gỗ lạnh. Một bà cụ ngồi ở đầu kia với một con chó nhỏ. Bà đọc báo rất chậm. Một người đàn ông mặc áo khoác đen đi đi lại lại. Anh ấy nhìn đồng hồ nhiều lần. Tàu trễ hai mươi phút. Tôi không thấy bực. Tôi cảm thấy bình yên một cách lạ. Tôi nhìn tuyết trên đường ray. Tôi nghe tiếng còi xa từ một con tàu khác. Khi tàu của tôi cuối cùng đến, ghế đã hơi ấm dưới chỗ tôi ngồi. Bà cụ gấp tờ báo lại. Người đàn ông ngừng đi. Tất cả chúng tôi cùng đứng dậy. Tôi sẽ nhớ sân ga yên tĩnh đó rất lâu.

options: To a small station outside Sofia
To a bus stop in the village
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I went to a small train station outside the city' (Sofia).

options: To visit a friend in a village two hours away
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I wanted to visit a friend in a village two hours away'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the reason in the second sentence.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Light snow was on the ground'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who else was on the platform?
options: An old woman with a small dog, and a man in a black coat
mcQuestions.explanation: The text describes both the old woman with a small dog and the man in a black coat.
mcQuestions.hint: There are two other people mentioned.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The train was twenty minutes late'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did the writer feel during the wait?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I felt strangely calm'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is correct past simple?
options: I goed to the station yesterday.
I went to the station yesterday.
I gone to the station yesterday.
I going to the station yesterday.

mcQuestions.hint: 'almost' goes right before the word it changes.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the old woman do at the end?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The old woman folded her newspaper'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the action when the train arrives.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple of 'read'.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'read' has the same spelling in past simple (pronounced /red/).

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why can a slow, uncomfortable wait sometimes become a memory we treasure for years?
